prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: on the left a calm tidy engineer at a single chalk circle holds one elegant glowing matrix shape that magically unfolds into a long road stretching to the horizon, while on the right a sweaty robot apprentice flips through a giant flipbook copying an expert dancer pose by pose, the two halves mirrored as two routes to the same confident stride forward, expressive faces, generous negative space, XKCD Kurzgesagt feel, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: On the left a calm engineer holds one glowing matrix that unfolds into a road to the horizon, representing optimal control solved in closed form; on the right a robot apprentice copies an expert dancer pose by pose from a flipbook, representing imitation learning, two routes to the same confident action.
caption: Two ways to act well in the world: solve the whole future as one equation, or learn by copying someone who already does.

after_heading: 2. The Discrete-Time Riccati Equation: Dynamic Programming in Closed Form
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: a serene robot sage sits cross legged having folded an enormously long accordion strip of future moments into a single small glowing cube resting calmly in its palm, the infinite road in the background collapsing fold by fold into that one cube, the sage looks utterly unworried with nothing left to compute, expressive peaceful face, generous negative space, XKCD Kurzgesagt feel, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A serene robot sage folds an enormously long accordion strip representing the infinite future into a single small glowing cube held in its palm, showing how the Riccati equation collapses the entire horizon into one constant matrix the controller reads off forever.
caption: The Riccati equation folds an infinite future into one constant, so the controller stops computing and simply multiplies.

after_heading: 1. The MPC Idea: Optimize a Horizon, Execute One Step, Replan
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: a hiker robot in fog plans a long careful dotted route to a distant peak with a small telescope, then takes exactly one confident step and immediately redraws the whole dotted route from the new spot, the previous plan fading behind it, the scene repeated lightly as a rhythm of plan step replan, expressive determined face, generous negative space, XKCD Kurzgesagt feel, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A hiker robot in fog plans a long dotted route to a peak with a telescope, takes a single step, then redraws the entire route from its new position, illustrating model predictive control which optimizes a full horizon but executes only one step before replanning.
caption: Model predictive control plans far ahead but commits to only the next step, then replans, which is what makes it shrug off surprises.

after_heading: 2. Behavioral Cloning: Supervised Learning of a Policy, and Its Hidden Trap
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: a student robot confidently traces an experts neat path drawn across a sheet of paper, but a small gust of wind has nudged it half a step into the blank margin where no path was ever drawn, and there it stands baffled and improvising while its footprints wander off into stranger and emptier whitespace, expressive lost but earnest face, generous negative space, XKCD Kurzgesagt feel, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A student robot traces an expert path across a sheet but a gust of wind pushes it into the blank margin where no path was drawn, and it wanders off baffled into empty whitespace, dramatizing behavioral cloning failing on states the expert never visited.
caption: Behavioral cloning copies the expert perfectly until one nudge lands it in unlabeled territory, where small errors compound into total drift.

after_heading: 3. DAgger: Labeling the Learner's Own States
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: a student robot wanders into the blank margin of a sheet and a friendly expert mentor leans in pointing the right way precisely there in the margin, adding a fresh footprint to the students own wandering trail so the messy detour becomes newly charted ground, the student visibly relieved and corrected, expressive grateful face, generous negative space, XKCD Kurzgesagt feel, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A student robot wanders into a blank margin and an expert mentor leans in to point the correct direction right there, adding labels to the learners own visited states, illustrating how DAgger fixes covariate shift by querying the expert on states the student actually reaches.
caption: DAgger fixes the drift by labeling the states the learner actually visits, so the expert teaches in exactly the margins cloning never saw.

after_heading: 1. Inverse Reinforcement Learning: Infer the Reward, Then Optimize It
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: a detective robot with a magnifying glass studies the elegant footprints of an expert across the ground and from them reconstructs a glowing compass needle revealing the hidden goal that must have driven every step, the detective sketching that compass mid air rather than just copying the footprints, expressive thoughtful aha face, generous negative space, XKCD Kurzgesagt feel, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A detective robot studies an experts footprints with a magnifying glass and reconstructs a glowing compass needle pointing to the hidden goal that explains the behavior, illustrating inverse reinforcement learning which infers the reward behind demonstrations rather than copying the actions.
caption: Inverse reinforcement learning asks not what the expert did but why, recovering the hidden reward that explains every move.
